items.title: De samenvattingen van de kwartfinales in de Eurojackpot KNVB Beker
items.summary: De halvefinalisten in de Eurojackpot KNVB Beker zijn bekend. Bekijk hier de samenvattingen van de vier wedstrijden in de kwartfinale die deze week werden afgewerkt. Vrijdagmiddag wordt er geloot voor de halve finale.

items.title: Sparta klaar voor strijd om Europees voetbal: 'Verdedigen nu als kerels'
items.summary: Sparta Rotterdam beleeft een uitstekend seizoen. De ploeg van Maurice Steijn heeft zich in de subtop genesteld en maakt een serieuze kans op Europees voetbal. En dat terwijl het aan het begin van het seizoen nog tegendoelpunten en harde nederlagen regende.

items.title: Kraker voor Barça en Baskische derby in halve finale Copa del Rey
items.summary: Barcelona stuit in de halve finale van het Spaanse bekertoernooi op Atlético Madrid. Dat heeft de loting vrijdagmiddag uitgewezen.

items.title: Oxlade-Chamberlain set to join Celtic, Slot praises Wirtz, Milner eyes record – football live
items.summary: ⚽ All the latest news heading into the weekend’s action ⚽ Premier League: 10 things to look out for | Mail John Quiz of the week: I guessed my way to a rather decent 12/16. Virgil van Dijk has hit out at ex-pro pundits in an interview with, er, Gary Neville, ahead of Sky’s broadcast of Liverpool v Manchester City. For me personally, I can deal with it, but I’m a bit worried for the next generation. I feel like the ex-top players have a responsibility to the new generation. Criticism is absolutely normal and part of the game, and I think it should stay that way. But sometimes criticism also goes into being clickbait, saying things to provoke things, and without thinking about the repercussions for a mental side of players, and especially the younger generation, who are constantly on social media. Continue reading...

items.title: 'Fantastische' Frenkie de Jong krijgt groot compliment: 'Hij is een leider'
items.summary: Hansi Flick heeft op de persconferentie in aanloop naar de wedstrijd tegen Real Mallorca een groot compliment gegeven aan Frenkie de Jong (28). De Barcelona-trainer vindt dat De Jong grote stappen gezet heeft in het nemen van verantwoordelijkheid.

items.title: Premier League Darts table 2026 - Latest standings as Humphries defends title
items.summary: Eight of the world's top players, including Luke Littler and Luke Humphries, will battle it out to be crowned Premier League Darts champion at London's O2 arena on May 28.

items.title: AZ treft Benfica in achtste finale Youth League
items.summary: AZ Onder-19 is bij de loting voor de de achtste finale van de UEFA Youth League gekoppeld aan Benfica. De club uit Alkmaar is de enige Nederlandse afvaardiging die nog actief is in het Europese jeugdtoernooi.

items.title: T20 World Cup quiz! How much do you know? Test your knowledge!
items.summary: As the 2026 ICC Men's T20 World Cup gets under way on Saturday February 7, we want to put your tournament knowledge to the ultimate test!

items.title: ‘I enjoy the Championship’: Burnley fans stay positive as relegation looms
items.summary: Clarets host must-win game against West Ham with club facing a third demotion in three Premier League seasons “I still enjoy the day out, I just don’t let the results affect me any more,” says the Burnley season-ticket holder Mark Bentley. Fifteen games without a Premier League win and 11 points adrift of safety, the Clarets are facing relegation the season after promotion for a third time in a row. Sparring with the best should bring glorious enjoyment at Turf Moor but instead they have three victories from 24 games, beating the two other promoted sides and rock-bottom Wolves, with survival chances looking worse than slim. Relegation rivals West Ham visit on Saturday, and another defeat would remove any faint hope that Burnley could turn it around and leave their record over recent seasons reading: down, up, down, up, down. Beating Sunderland in the first home game brought optimism after a summer where money was spent, notably bringing in the 96-cap Kyle Walker after eight years of success at Manchester City, but that feelgood factor has dwindled. Continue reading...

items.title: Saudi league warns Ronaldo over transfer protest
items.summary: Cristiano Ronaldo was told by the Saudi Pro League that "no individual -- however significant -- determines decisions beyond their own club" amid a dispute over transfer spending.

items.title: Utd's Carrick relishes reunion with Spurs boss
items.summary: Michael Carrick finds himself up against Thomas Frank just five months on from interviewing the Tottenham boss.

items.title: Source: Minnesota finalizing James Rodríguez deal
items.summary: Minnesota United is finalizing a deal to sign Colombia international star forward James Rodríguez, a source confirmed to ESPN.

items.title: Leicester deducted 6 points for P&S rule breach
items.summary: Championship side Leicester City have been deducted six points after being found to have breached the EFL's profit and sustainability rules for the 2023/24 season.

items.title: Settled Xavi Simons finds his groove at Spurs after time spent in the shadows
items.summary: Dutch midfielder has rubbed shoulders with Messi, Neymar and Mbappé but is now making his own name For Xavi Simons, it felt like a point of no return. He and his Tottenham teammates had nothing more to lose. The FA Cup tie at home to Aston Villa on 10 January was going badly. Played off the park by Unai Emery’s team, they were booed off by their own fans at half-time. They were losing 2-0. Simons takes it personally when things are not going well and that had been the case, pretty much, since his £51.8m move from RB Leipzig last August. The 22-year-old knows his levels. These were not them. Continue reading...

items.title: Man Utd vs Tottenham preview: Van de Ven back - but will Romero feature?
'Why young British managers need lower league experience'
items.summary: In his BBC Sport column, ex-Premier League boss Tony Pulis explains why he thinks there is a negative reaction to British managers when they get jobs in the Premier League - and the path young coaches should take.

items.title: Three years on: what is the latest with Premier League charges against Manchester City? | Paul MacInnes
items.summary: In February 2023 more than 100 charges were laid against the serial champions but we are still waiting for the verdict Exactly three years ago, 10 paragraphs on the Premier League website set the cat among the pigeons. Under the nondescript heading “Premier League statement”, football’s richest and most popular domestic competition announced unprecedented disciplinary charges against Manchester City, champions of the two previous seasons (and the two to come). We are still waiting for the outcome. The estimated 134 charges covered years of alleged wrongdoing but broke down into a couple of key chunks: accusations that City had failed to provide “accurate financial information” to the league and to properly “cooperate … and assist” with the subsequent investigation. Precious little new information has followed. Continue reading...

items.title: Ex-players have 'duty of responsibility' as pundits - Van Dijk
items.summary: Former players turned pundits have a "duty of responsibility" to the new generation of footballers when commenting on their performances, Liverpool captain Virgil van Dijk says.

items.title: Carrick has restored hope to Man United, but it's what comes next that matters
items.summary: Michael Carrick's three-game winning streak has certainly lifted the mood at Old Trafford. But building on this early form will truly test Man United's mettle.

items.title: How Casemiro revived his Man Utd career
items.summary: Casemiro's Manchester United career looked to be petering out, but the veteran midfielder has found a return to form this season.
